_comment: Binds the committed SP1 guest ELF to the verifying keys it must be deployed with. verify-vkey-pin.sh asserts sha256(program/elf/teth-pool-prover) == elf_sha256 (always) and, where the SP1 toolchain is present, that the vkey derived from the ELF == program_vkey. A guest rebuild that changes the ELF MUST regenerate the vkey and update this file IN THE SAME COMMIT, then redeploy SP1PoolRootVerifier with the new PROGRAM_VKEY — otherwise every proveStateTransition reverts and all withdrawals brick.
guest_state: HARDENED — denom canonical/pairwise-distinct asserts + CXFER checked_add. NON-DOCKER build (built on the vast prover host via `cargo prove build`, not `--docker`), so this vkey is reproducible only by deriving from this exact committed ELF, not by an independent rebuild. Fine for the single-prover signet pilot; recompile canonically with `cargo prove build --docker` and re-pin before permissionless mainnet provers.
